title: Structs 1
description: Structs in Rust allow you to group related data together. Classic structs have named fields.
instructions: Complete the struct definition and instantiation.
hints: A struct field is defined as `field_name: Type`.
The fields should be `red`, `green`, and `blue`, each of type `u8` or `i32`.
Add: red: i32, green: i32, blue: i32 inside the struct body.
explanation: Classic structs in Rust have named fields, each with a type. You create instances by specifying values for all fields using `StructName { field: value }` syntax. Fields are accessed with dot notation. Structs are the primary way to create custom data types in Rust.
